title: T-077 — refactor grantRole from BYPASSRLS owner pool to getAppConnection + set_org_context (FT-017)
prompt:
# T-077 — refactor grantRole from BYPASSRLS owner pool to getAppConnection + set_org_context (FT-017)

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5, §5.x
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
Original carry-forward ID: **FT-017** (from `_meta/audits/2026-05-07-foundation-final-carry-forward-backlog.md`)
Severity: **P1** (M effort)

## Goal
grantRole runs under the app-role + set_org_context — owner-pool removed.

## Implementation contract
1. Replace `getOwnerPool()` with `getAppConnection(orgId)` from packages/db.
2. First statement in transaction: `SELECT set_org_context($1)`.
3. Remove all BYPASSRLS escalations.
4. Integration test asserts a same-org grant succeeds and a forged cross-org attempt is rejected by RLS (SQLSTATE 42501).

## Files (scope)
- packages/rbac/src/grant.ts [modify]
- packages/rbac/src/__tests__/grant-rls.integration.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given grantRole is called for a same-org grant, when it runs, then the connection is on the app-role and the grant succeeds.
2. Given a forged cross-org grant attempt (bypassing the FT-016 assertion in a test harness), when it runs, then RLS denies it with SQLSTATE 42501.
3. Given the audit_events row for the successful grant, when inspected, then org_id matches the target org.

## Test strategy
- RED: write grant-rls.integration.test.ts asserting RLS denial before refactor.
- Pin SQLSTATE 42501.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/rbac test grant-rls`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not retain any owner-pool escape hatch in grantRole.
- Do not skip set_org_context.
- Do not use raw new pg.Pool.

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED-first) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Refactor grantRole to use getAppConnection() + set_org_context() instead of the BYPASSRLS owner pool, removing the silent-cross-org-leak risk surface.
pipeline_inputs.details: grantRole currently opens an owner-pool connection that bypasses RLS. Move to the app-role connection with `select set_org_context($1)` so cross-org writes are denied by RLS as well as by the FT-016 assertion.
out_of_scope: Do not refactor other rbac functions in this task (separate FTs).
acceptance_criteria: Given grantRole is called for a same-org grant, when it runs, then the connection is on the app-role and the grant succeeds.
Given a forged cross-org grant attempt (bypassing the FT-016 assertion in a test harness), when it runs, then RLS denies it with SQLSTATE 42501.
Given the audit_events row for the successful grant, when inspected, then org_id matches the target org.
test_strategy: RED: write grant-rls.integration.test.ts asserting RLS denial before refactor.
risk_red_lines: Do not retain any owner-pool escape hatch in grantRole.
Do not use raw new pg.Pool.
